entries.title: Tech Stack Fingerprint Pro
entries.notes: Analyzes HTTP headers, script tags, CDN fingerprints, and meta tags to identify frameworks, CDNs, analytics, and hosting. One engineer can build the detection rules engine in 2-3 weeks. Wappalyzer exists but lacks a lightweight sidebar overlay and freemium model for solo devs.
entries.pain: Wasting time reverse-engineering what stack a prospect's site uses before outreach or debugging.

entries.title: Feature Flag Health Reporter
entries.notes: Connects to LaunchDarkly/Flagsmith/etc API, surfaces flags not toggled in 30+ days, flags with contradictory rules, and generates a weekly digest. One API integration + dashboard in ~3 weeks. Competitors like Harness have enterprise tooling but nothing lightweight.
entries.pain: Feature flags pile up silently until nobody knows which ones are stale, active, or causing conflicts.

entries.title: Webhook Signature Debugger
entries.notes: Intercepts webhook requests, runs real-time HMAC verification against stored secrets, shows pass/fail with hex diffs. Ships as DevTools panel + local secret store. Built in 1-2 weeks. NGROK has tunnel inspect but no signature verification layer.
entries.pain: Webhook signature validation failures waste hours debugging payloads and shared secrets.

entries.title: Page Resource Waterfall Analyzer
entries.notes: Parses Performance API entries, renders a cleaner waterfall, highlights resources >1s and flags render-blocking scripts. Exports as PNG/PDF for sprint reports. Built in 2 weeks. Fullstory and Datadog offer this in paid APM but not a standalone free DevTools panel.
entries.pain: Identifying slow resources in DevTools waterfall charts is tedious and not shareable with non-technical stakeholders.

entries.title: Stripe Event Schema Validator
entries.notes: Validates incoming Stripe webhook payloads against the latest API schema version, flags unknown fields and type mismatches. Uses Stripe's public OpenAPI spec. One engineer, 2 weeks. Postman has some validation but not browser-native.
entries.pain: Debugging Stripe webhook failures means manually comparing payloads against docs with no real-time validation.

entries.title: AI Token Usage by Tab
entries.notes: Proxies LLM API calls via background script, tracks tokens per domain/tab in real-time, shows running cost totals. OpenAI/Anthropic billing APIs enable this. Built in 2 weeks. Cursor has token counters but not cross-platform tab tracking.
entries.pain: Running ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini simultaneously makes it impossible to track cumulative spend.

entries.title: CSV Column Profile Generator
entries.notes: Intercepts downloads, parses CSV in a Web Worker, shows data types, null percentages, min/max, and value distributions before saving. Works on any site. Built in 1-2 weeks. No browser extension does this as a universal download interceptor.
entries.pain: Downloading CSVs with no schema documentation means wasted time figuring out what columns actually contain.

entries.title: Support Sentiment Trend Monitor
entries.notes: Integrates with Zendesk/Intercom API, runs lightweight sentiment scoring on ticket text over time, surfaces weekly digest of declining CSAT and rising negative keywords. Built in 3 weeks. Gainsight exists but overkill for 10-50 seat SMBs.
entries.pain: Support teams see tickets individually but miss macro trends indicating product friction or churn risk.

entries.title: Meet Engagement Pulse
entries.notes: Analyzes Google Meet transcript for question frequency, response latency, and sentiment per participant. Generates engagement score per attendee. Uses Meet API + basic NLP. Built in 3 weeks. Gong has this but at enterprise pricing.
entries.pain: Sales reps can't prove prospects were engaged during demos unless they manually review recordings.

entries.title: Notion Backlink Graph Viewer
entries.notes: Uses Notion API to index all page links, renders a force-directed graph showing connections. Highlights orphan pages. Built in 2 weeks. Notion itself shows backlinks but not a visual graph view.
entries.pain: Notion wikis grow into invisible webs where you can't tell what links to what without clicking every page.

entries.title: Sponsored Content Radar
entries.notes: Detects ad indicators in Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Reddit DOM (promoted tags, sponsor labels, paid partnerships). Toggles a visual badge on flagged posts. Built in 1 week. Browser-native content detection is lightweight.
entries.pain: Social feeds mix organic and sponsored posts invisibly, making it hard to know what you're actually reading.

entries.title: GitHub Codeowner Coverage Checker
entries.notes: Scans repo for paths not covered by CODEOWNERS, flags glob patterns that match no files, surfaces reviewers with too many assignments. Runs on GitHub repo pages. Built in 2 weeks. No tool does this as a lightweight browser panel.
entries.pain: CODEOWNERS files silently decay until PRs get stuck because nobody is actually responsible.

entries.title: Browser Session Cost Attribution
entries.notes: Tracks active tab time per domain, maps against known SaaS pricing, generates weekly spend attribution report. Uses idle detection API. Built in 2 weeks. Toggl has time tracking but no cost mapping to tool pricing.
entries.pain: Engineering leads can't justify SaaS tool spend without per-developer usage breakdowns.

entries.title: Proxy Config Proxy Switcher
entries.notes: One-click switch between saved proxy profiles, auto-rotate user agents per profile, logs requests through active proxy. Built in 2 weeks. Proxyman exists but desktop-only with no one-click browser switching.
entries.pain: Switching between multiple proxy configurations for testing requires digging into OS settings every time.

entries.title: Job Posting Tech Signal Extractor
entries.notes: Scrapes LinkedIn/Indeed job descriptions, extracts tech keywords (AWS, React, Postgres), builds a quick tech profile for the target company. Built in 2 weeks. LinkedIn Sales Navigator has some signals but not tech stack extraction.
entries.pain: Scoping enterprise sales deals by manually reading job postings to guess tech stack is slow and inconsistent.
